brief.objective: Assess where strategic value is concentrating as model quality converges.
constraints: Prefer strategic interpretation over generic recap.
Keep output useful under time pressure.

sources.title: Model vendors are racing to own the application layer
sources.summary: Product vendors are bundling AI capabilities into broader suites rather than selling isolated features.

sources.title: Enterprise buyers care less about frontier prestige and more about workflow fit
sources.summary: Adoption correlates more strongly with embedded workflow value than with headline model quality.

sources.title: Context, memory, and interface are emerging as sticky advantages
sources.summary: The winning surface may be where user context accumulates rather than where raw model quality peaks.

signals.statement: Distribution is carrying more strategic weight as raw model differentiation narrows.
signals.evidence: Multiple product and buyer signals point toward packaging, workflow integration, and reach becoming more decisive than standalone model access.
implications: Owning the user relationship becomes economically more important.
Go-to-market strength can compound technical capability rather than merely follow it.
counterpoints: Frontier model gaps can still matter in narrow, high-stakes domains.

signals.statement: Context capture is emerging as the scarcer asset than inference alone.
signals.evidence: Embedded systems that learn user habits and store reusable context are harder to displace than point solutions that only answer prompts.
implications: Memory and workflow embedding can become part of the moat.
Switching costs may rise at the interface layer rather than the model layer.

signals.statement: Interface design is increasingly shaping product economics, not just usability.
signals.evidence: The interface determines what gets repeated, measured, remembered, and eventually monetized.
implications: The presentation surface influences retention and monetization.
UI decisions can determine where strategic capture lands.
signals.notes: This signal matters less as a headline and more as a mechanism behind the broader shift.

synthesis.workingThesis: As raw model capability converges, the battle for value is moving up the stack toward workflow ownership, context capture, and distribution.
supportingThemes: workflow control is a strategic surface
context accumulates where users return daily
openQuestions: Which layer keeps the recurring user relationship as model access broadens?
Where will the most valuable non-portable context accumulate?
contradictions: Some categories will still reward raw model leadership more than distribution depth.
missingInformation: A stronger set of examples from vertical software categories would improve confidence in the workflow thesis.

editorialDecisions.heroFrame: The strategic fight is shifting from who has the best model to who owns the workflow around it.
editorialDecisions.topLineThesis: Distribution is becoming as important as the model.
watchlistSeeds: Which layer captures the recurring user relationship?
What kinds of context become least portable over time?
Which products turn occasional use into operational dependency?
